gaps.title: Universal NPU Abstraction Layer
gaps.description: No framework provides a single API that abstracts across ALL mobile NPUs (Qualcomm Hexagon, Apple Neural Engine, MediaTek APU, Samsung Exynos NPU, Google Tensor TPU). Developers must use vendor-specific SDKs or rely on ExecuTorch's limited backend coverage. A HAL (hardware abstraction layer) for NPU-accelerated LLM inference would let developers write once and deploy to any NPU.
gaps.current_state: ExecuTorch supports 12+ backends but requires per-backend configuration. QNN, Core ML, and Samsung AI Studio are siloed. No single tool targets all NPUs with zero-config.
gaps.target_users: Mobile app developers deploying LLMs across Android + iOS

gaps.title: On-Device RAG Pipeline Framework
gaps.description: Almost no on-device inference tool includes built-in retrieval-augmented generation. Cactus has 'auto RAG' but it's proprietary and limited. There's no open-source, on-device RAG pipeline that handles local vector storage, document chunking, embedding, and retrieval—all running on-device without cloud dependency.
gaps.current_state: Cactus is the only tool with built-in RAG. Others require separate vector DB (which don't exist for mobile) plus custom integration.
gaps.target_users: Enterprise mobile app developers needing private document Q&A

gaps.title: Automated Hardware-Aware Model Selection & Optimization
gaps.description: Developers must manually choose which model, quantization level, and runtime to use for each target device. No tool automatically profiles the device hardware (RAM, NPU type, thermal budget) and selects/optimizes the best model configuration. This is a critical workflow gap for apps targeting diverse Android devices.
gaps.current_state: Manual benchmarking required. LiteRT-LM does some auto-selection for Gemini Nano, but nothing exists for open models.
gaps.target_users: Android developers targeting 1000+ device SKUs

gaps.title: On-Device Reasoning Chain Debugger/Visualizer
gaps.description: No tool provides visibility into how an on-device SLM reasons through chain-of-thought. Cloud providers have tracing tools, but nothing exists for debugging reasoning quality, token attribution, or inference paths on-device. Critical for safety-sensitive edge deployments (automotive, medical).
gaps.current_state: Zero tooling. Developers log raw outputs and guess what went wrong.
gaps.target_users: Safety-critical edge AI developers (automotive, medical, industrial)

gaps.title: Cross-Platform Mobile LLM SDK (React Native / Flutter / KMP)
gaps.description: Only Cactus offers native bindings for React Native, Flutter, and Kotlin Multiplatform. No open-source alternative exists. The majority of mobile apps are built with cross-platform frameworks, yet most LLM runtimes only offer C/C++ or platform-native APIs.
gaps.current_state: Cactus (commercial, proprietary format). llama.cpp has community React Native wrappers but they're fragile. ExecuTorch has no cross-platform mobile bindings.
gaps.target_users: Cross-platform mobile developers (80%+ of mobile dev workforce)

gaps.title: On-Device Model Fine-Tuning / Personalization
gaps.description: No production tool supports fine-tuning or LoRA adaptation directly on-device. Models are static after deployment. Users can't personalize models with their own data without sending it to the cloud, which defeats the privacy benefits of on-device inference.
gaps.current_state: MLX supports fine-tuning on Mac but not mobile. LiteRT-LM supports LoRA switching but not training. No mobile fine-tuning exists.
gaps.target_users: Privacy-focused enterprises, personalized AI assistant developers

gaps.title: OTA Model Lifecycle Management Platform
gaps.description: Managing model versions, A/B testing models, rolling back bad model deploys, and monitoring model performance across device fleets—no open-source platform exists for this. Only Cactus has OTA updates, and Google manages it internally for Gemini Nano.
gaps.current_state: Cactus (proprietary OTA). Google internal for Gemini. No open MLOps-for-edge platform.
gaps.target_users: Mobile teams deploying LLMs to millions of devices

gaps.title: Hybrid Edge-Cloud Inference Orchestrator
gaps.description: When an on-device SLM can't handle a query (too complex, out of domain), it should seamlessly escalate to a cloud LLM. Only Cactus and Apple (Private Cloud Compute) do this. No open-source tool provides intelligent routing between on-device and cloud inference based on query complexity, latency budget, or cost.
gaps.current_state: Cactus has basic cloud fallback. Apple has Private Cloud Compute. No open solution with smart routing.
gaps.target_users: Enterprise developers needing reliability guarantees

gaps.title: Edge-Optimized Reasoning Model Distillation Toolkit
gaps.description: Distilling reasoning capabilities from frontier models (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini) into edge-deployable SLMs is a known technique but there's no turnkey toolkit. Developers must manually design distillation pipelines, select teacher-student pairs, and validate reasoning quality.
gaps.current_state: Research papers exist. DeepSeek, Qwen do it internally. No open toolkit for custom reasoning distillation targeting specific edge use cases.
gaps.target_users: ML engineers building domain-specific reasoning SLMs

gaps.title: On-Device Multi-Agent Orchestration
gaps.description: Agentic AI is trending but no on-device framework supports running multiple specialized SLMs (one for reasoning, one for coding, one for retrieval) and orchestrating them locally. All agent frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI) are cloud-centric.
gaps.current_state: No on-device agent orchestration exists. Ollama can swap models but not run them concurrently with orchestration.
gaps.target_users: Developers building complex on-device AI assistants

gaps.title: Standardized Edge LLM Benchmarking Suite
gaps.description: No standardized benchmark suite exists for comparing on-device LLM inference across devices, runtimes, and models. Developers can't easily answer: 'Which runtime gives me the best tok/s on a Pixel 9 for Phi-4 mini with INT4?' MLPerf covers traditional ML but not LLM-specific metrics.
gaps.current_state: Fragmented benchmarks. Individual papers compare 2-3 tools. No comprehensive, reproducible suite.
gaps.target_users: All edge LLM developers and decision-makers

gaps.title: Wearable/IoT LLM Deployment (Ultra-Constrained)
gaps.description: Smartwatches, earbuds, AR glasses, and IoT devices have 256MB-1GB RAM and minimal compute. Only ExecuTorch targets MCUs, and Google deployed Gemini Nano on Pixel Watch, but no general-purpose framework makes it easy to deploy SLMs on ultra-constrained wearables.
gaps.current_state: ExecuTorch can target MCUs but with significant engineering. Cactus claims wearable support. No turnkey wearable LLM SDK.
gaps.target_users: Wearable device makers, AR/VR developers

gaps.title: Privacy-Certified On-Device Inference Framework
gaps.description: Healthcare, finance, and government need on-device inference with formal privacy guarantees (HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR certifications). No framework offers certified privacy controls, audit logs, data isolation, or compliance documentation for on-device LLM inference.
gaps.current_state: Apple touts privacy but no formal certification. All others rely on 'data stays on device' claim without formal guarantees.
gaps.target_users: Healthcare, fintech, and government AI developers

gaps.title: On-Device Structured Output / JSON Mode Guarantees
gaps.description: Cloud LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) offer guaranteed JSON output and structured responses. On-device runtimes have minimal or no structured output enforcement. This is critical for edge apps that need reliable parsing of LLM outputs.
gaps.current_state: WebLLM and llama.cpp server have basic JSON mode. No constrained decoding on mobile runtimes. No grammar-based output enforcement on NPU-accelerated paths.
gaps.target_users: Enterprise app developers needing reliable LLM output parsing

gaps.title: Edge LLM Monitoring & Observability Platform
gaps.description: No tool provides production monitoring for on-device LLM inference: latency percentiles, token throughput, error rates, model quality degradation, thermal throttling detection, battery impact tracking across a fleet of devices.
gaps.current_state: Zero tooling. Developers build custom logging. No edge-LLM-specific observability platform.
gaps.target_users: Mobile ops teams managing LLM-powered apps at scale

gaps.title: Offline-First Conversational AI SDK
gaps.description: Field workers, military, disaster response, rural areas—many users need conversational AI that works 100% offline with persistent context, local knowledge base, and multi-turn conversation. No SDK is optimized for this use case end-to-end.
gaps.current_state: Possible to cobble together with llama.cpp + manual persistence, but no integrated offline-first SDK.
gaps.target_users: Military, field service, disaster response, rural connectivity

gaps.title: Cross-Device Model Sharing & Federated Edge Inference
gaps.description: No tool supports splitting model inference across multiple edge devices (e.g., phone + laptop + smart speaker collaborating on a single query) or federated learning across edge devices.
gaps.current_state: Research papers on split inference exist (Petals, etc.) but nothing production-ready for consumer edge devices.
gaps.target_users: Smart home developers, enterprise campus deployments

gaps.title: Developer-Friendly Model Conversion & Optimization Hub
gaps.description: Converting a HuggingFace model to run on-device requires navigating GGUF, ONNX, Core ML, ExecuTorch .pte, TensorRT, QNN formats—each with different tools and gotchas. No unified conversion/optimization hub exists that takes any HuggingFace model and produces optimized artifacts for all target runtimes.
gaps.current_state: Each runtime has its own converter. HuggingFace Optimum helps but doesn't cover all targets. Manual, error-prone process.
gaps.target_users: ML engineers deploying models across diverse edge targets

gaps.title: Automotive/Robotics Edge LLM Integration SDK
gaps.description: TensorRT-Edge-LLM targets automotive but is NVIDIA-only. No framework-agnostic SDK exists for integrating LLMs into automotive (AUTOSAR, ROS2) or robotics (ROS2) middleware with real-time safety guarantees, sensor fusion, and deterministic latency.
gaps.current_state: TensorRT-Edge-LLM + Bosch for NVIDIA Jetson. Nothing for Qualcomm/ARM-based automotive platforms.
gaps.target_users: Automotive OEMs, robotics companies

gaps.title: Free/Open Model Marketplace with Edge-Optimized Artifacts
gaps.description: HuggingFace has models but few are pre-optimized for specific edge runtimes. No marketplace offers pre-quantized, pre-compiled, benchmarked model artifacts for each runtime+device combination, with performance guarantees and one-click deployment.
gaps.current_state: HuggingFace has GGUF models. Ollama has a model library. But no edge-specific marketplace with per-device benchmarks and optimized artifacts for ExecuTorch, LiteRT, QNN, etc.
gaps.target_users: All edge LLM developers
